title: List Creatives Response
description: Response from creative library query with filtered results, metadata, and optional enriched data

query_summary.description: Summary of the query that was executed

total_matching.description: Total number of creatives matching filters (across all pages)

returned.description: Number of creatives returned in this response

filters_applied.description: List of filters that were applied to the query

pagination.title: Pagination Response
pagination.description: Standard cursor-based pagination metadata for list responses

has_more.description: Whether more results are available beyond this page

cursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass in the next request to fetch the next page. Only present when has_more is true.

total_count.description: Total number of items matching the query across all pages. Optional because not all backends can efficiently compute this.

creatives.description: Array of creative assets matching the query

advertiser.description: The advertiser whose rates apply to this account

billing_proxy.description: Optional intermediary who receives invoices on behalf of the advertiser (e.g., agency)

status.title: Account Status
status.description: Account lifecycle status. See the Accounts Protocol overview for the operations matrix showing which tasks are permitted in each state.

enumDescriptions.active: Account is operational and can create media buys
enumDescriptions.pending_approval: Seller is reviewing the account (credit, contracts)
enumDescriptions.rejected: Seller declined the account request — terminal state
enumDescriptions.payment_required: Credit limit reached or funds depleted
enumDescriptions.suspended: Was active, now paused — operations gated
enumDescriptions.closed: Was active, now terminated — terminal state

domain.description: Domain where /.well-known/brand.json is hosted, or the brand's operating domain

brand_id.title: Brand ID
brand_id.description: Brand identifier within the house portfolio. Optional for single-brand domains.

operator.description: Domain of the entity operating this account. When the brand operates directly, this is the brand's domain.

billing.description: Who is invoiced on this account. operator: seller invoices the operator (agency or brand buying direct). agent: agent consolidates billing. advertiser: seller invoices the advertiser directly, even when a different operator places orders on their behalf. See billing_entity for the invoiced party's business details.

billing_entity.title: Business Entity
billing_entity.description: Business entity details for the party responsible for payment. Contains the legal name, tax IDs, address, and bank details needed for formal B2B invoicing. Corresponds to whoever billing points to (operator, agent, or advertiser). When this account appears in a response, bank details MUST be omitted (write-only).

legal_name.description: Registered legal name of the business entity

vat_id.description: VAT identification number (e.g., DE123456789 for Germany, FR12345678901 for France). Required for B2B invoicing in the EU. Must be normalized: no spaces, dots, or dashes.

tax_id.description: Tax identification number for jurisdictions that do not use VAT (e.g., US EIN)

registration_number.description: Company registration number (e.g., HRB 12345 for German Handelsregister)

address.description: Postal address for invoicing and legal correspondence

contacts.description: Contacts for billing, legal, and operational matters. Contains personal data subject to GDPR and equivalent regulations. Implementations MUST use this data only for invoicing and account management.

enumDescriptions.general: Default contact when no specific role applies

role.description: Contact's functional role in the business relationship

bank.description: Bank account details for payment processing. Write-only: included in requests to provide payment coordinates, but MUST NOT be echoed in responses. Sellers store these details and confirm receipt without returning them.

bic.description: Bank Identifier Code / SWIFT code (SEPA markets)

routing_number.description: Bank routing number for non-SEPA markets (e.g., US ABA routing number, Canadian transit/institution number)

account_number.description: Bank account number for non-SEPA markets

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

examples.description: German agency with full B2B details
US advertiser with EIN and domestic bank details

rate_card.description: Identifier for the rate card applied to this account

payment_terms.description: Payment terms agreed for this account. Binding for all invoices when the account is active.

setup.description: Present when status is 'pending_approval'. Contains next steps for completing account activation.

url.description: URL where the human can complete the required action (credit application, legal agreement, add funds).

account_scope.description: How the seller scoped this account. operator: shared across all brands for this operator. brand: shared across all operators for this brand. operator_brand: dedicated to a specific operator+brand combination. agent: the agent's default account with no brand or operator association.

governance_agents.description: Governance agent endpoints registered on this account. Authentication credentials are write-only and not included in responses — use sync_governance to set or update credentials.

url.description: Governance agent endpoint URL. Must use HTTPS.

categories.description: Governance categories this agent handles (e.g., ['budget_authority', 'strategic_alignment']). When omitted, the agent handles all categories.

sandbox.description: When true, this is a sandbox account — no real platform calls, no real spend. For explicit accounts (require_operator_auth: true), sandbox accounts are pre-existing test accounts on the platform discovered via list_accounts. For implicit accounts, sandbox is part of the natural key: the same brand/operator pair can have both a production and sandbox account.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

examples.description: Direct advertiser account
Advertiser account with agency billing proxy
Agency as direct buyer
Account with brand identity and operator (via sync_accounts)
Pending account awaiting seller approval
Agency operates but advertiser is billed directly with structured billing entity

format_id.title: Format ID
format_id.description: Format identifier specifying which format this creative conforms to

agent_url.description: URL of the agent that defines this format (e.g., 'https://creatives.adcontextprotocol.org' for standard formats, or 'https://publisher.com/.well-known/adcp/sales' for custom formats)

id.description: Format identifier within the agent's namespace (e.g., 'display_static', 'video_hosted', 'audio_standard'). When used alone, references a template format. When combined with dimension/duration fields, creates a parameterized format ID for a specific variant.

width.description: Width in pixels for visual formats. When specified, height must also be specified. Both fields together create a parameterized format ID for dimension-specific variants.

height.description: Height in pixels for visual formats. When specified, width must also be specified. Both fields together create a parameterized format ID for dimension-specific variants.

duration_ms.description: Duration in milliseconds for time-based formats (video, audio). When specified, creates a parameterized format ID. Omit to reference a template format without parameters.

status.title: Creative Status
status.description: Current approval status of the creative

enumDescriptions.processing: Creative is being ingested, transcoded, or validated. Automatically transitions to pending_review when processing completes, or to rejected if processing fails (e.g., corrupt file, unsupported codec).
enumDescriptions.pending_review: Creative has passed processing and is awaiting platform content policy review. Transitions to approved or rejected after review.
enumDescriptions.approved: Creative has been approved and is eligible for delivery. May be archived by the buyer or re-reviewed by the seller.
enumDescriptions.rejected: Creative was rejected due to content policy or technical issues. Not terminal — the buyer may fix the issue and resubmit via sync_creatives, which returns the creative to processing.
enumDescriptions.archived: Creative has been archived by the buyer and is excluded from default queries. Archived creatives retain their data and may be unarchived back to approved.

updated_date.description: When the creative was last modified

assets.description: Assets for this creative, keyed by asset_id

anyOf.title: Image Asset
anyOf.description: Image asset with URL and dimensions

format.description: Image file format (jpg, png, gif, webp, etc.)

provenance.title: Provenance
provenance.description: Provenance metadata for this asset, overrides manifest-level provenance

digital_source_type.title: Digital Source Type
digital_source_type.description: IPTC-aligned classification of AI involvement in producing this content

enumDescriptions.digital_capture: Captured by a digital device (camera, scanner, screen recording) with no AI involvement
enumDescriptions.digital_creation: Created by a human using digital tools (Photoshop, Illustrator, After Effects) without AI generation
enumDescriptions.trained_algorithmic_media: Generated entirely by a trained AI model (DALL-E, Midjourney, Stable Diffusion, Sora)
enumDescriptions.composite_with_trained_algorithmic_media: Human-created content combined with AI-generated elements (e.g., photo with AI background)
enumDescriptions.algorithmic_media: Produced by deterministic algorithms without machine learning (procedural generation, rule-based systems)
enumDescriptions.composite_capture: Multiple digital captures composited together without AI
enumDescriptions.composite_synthetic: Composite of multiple elements where at least one is AI-generated (e.g., stock photo composited with AI-generated background)
enumDescriptions.human_edits: Content augmented, corrected, or enhanced by humans using non-generative tools
enumDescriptions.data_driven_media: Assembled from structured data feeds (DCO templates, product catalogs, weather-triggered variants)

ai_tool.description: AI system used to generate or modify this content. Aligns with IPTC 2025.1 AI metadata fields and C2PA claim_generator.

name.description: Name of the AI tool or model (e.g., 'DALL-E 3', 'Stable Diffusion XL', 'Gemini')

version.description: Version identifier for the AI tool or model (e.g., '25.1', '0125', '2.1'). For generative models, use the model version rather than the API version.

provider.description: Organization that provides the AI tool (e.g., 'OpenAI', 'Stability AI', 'Google')

human_oversight.description: Level of human involvement in the AI-assisted creation process

enumDescriptions.none: Fully automated with no human involvement in generation
enumDescriptions.prompt_only: Human provided the prompt or instructions but did not review outputs
enumDescriptions.selected: Human selected from multiple AI-generated candidates
enumDescriptions.edited: Human edited or refined AI-generated output
enumDescriptions.directed: Human directed the creative process with AI as an assistive tool

declared_by.description: Party declaring this provenance. Identifies who attached the provenance claim, enabling receiving parties to assess trust.

agent_url.description: URL of the agent or service that declared this provenance

role.description: Role of the declaring party in the supply chain

enumDescriptions.creator: The party that created or generated the content
enumDescriptions.advertiser: The brand or advertiser that owns the content
enumDescriptions.agency: Agency acting on behalf of the advertiser
enumDescriptions.platform: Ad platform or publisher that processed the content
enumDescriptions.tool: Automated tool or service that attached provenance metadata

declared_at.description: When this provenance claim was made (ISO 8601). Distinct from created_time, which records when the content itself was produced. A provenance claim may be attached well after content creation, for example when retroactively declaring AI involvement for regulatory compliance.

created_time.description: When this content was created or generated (ISO 8601)

c2pa.description: C2PA Content Credentials reference. Links to the cryptographic provenance manifest for this content. Because file-level C2PA bindings break during ad-tech transcoding, this URL reference preserves the chain of provenance through the supply chain.

manifest_url.description: URL to the C2PA manifest store for this content

disclosure.description: Regulatory disclosure requirements for this content. Indicates whether AI disclosure is required and under which jurisdictions.

required.description: Whether AI disclosure is required for this content based on applicable regulations

region.description: Sub-national region code (e.g., 'CA' for California, 'BY' for Bavaria)

label_text.description: Required disclosure label text for this jurisdiction, in the local language

render_guidance.description: How the disclosure should be rendered for this jurisdiction. Expresses the declaring party's intent for persistence and position based on regulatory requirements. Publishers control actual rendering but governance agents can audit whether guidance was followed.

persistence.title: Disclosure Persistence
persistence.description: How long the disclosure must persist during content playback or display

enumDescriptions.continuous: Disclosure must remain visible or audible throughout the entire content display duration. For video and audio, this means the full playback duration. For static formats (display, DOOH), this means the full display slot. For DOOH specifically, 'content duration' means the ad's display slot within the rotation, not the screen's full rotation cycle.
enumDescriptions.initial: Disclosure must appear at the start of content for a minimum duration before it may be removed. Pair with min_duration_ms in render_guidance or creative brief to specify the required duration.
enumDescriptions.flexible: Disclosure presence is sufficient; placement timing and duration are at the publisher's discretion

min_duration_ms.description: Minimum display duration in milliseconds for initial persistence. Recommended when persistence is initial — without it, the duration is at the publisher's discretion. At serve time the publisher reads this from provenance since the brief is not available.

positions.description: Preferred disclosure positions in priority order. The first position a format supports should be used.

items.title: Disclosure Position
items.description: Where a required disclosure should appear within a creative. Used by creative briefs to specify disclosure placement and by formats to declare which positions they can render.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

verification.description: Third-party verification or detection results for this content. Multiple services may independently evaluate the same content. Provenance is a claim — verification results attached by the declaring party are supplementary. The enforcing party (e.g., seller/publisher) should run its own verification via get_creative_features or calibrate_content.

verified_by.description: Name of the verification service (e.g., 'DoubleVerify', 'Hive Moderation', 'Reality Defender')

confidence.description: Confidence score of the verification result (0.0 to 1.0)

details_url.description: URL to the full verification report

ext.title: Extension Object

anyOf.title: Video Asset
anyOf.description: Video asset with URL and technical specifications including audio track properties

container_format.description: Video container format (mp4, webm, mov, etc.)

video_codec.description: Video codec used (h264, h265, vp9, av1, prores, etc.)

video_bitrate_kbps.description: Video stream bitrate in kilobits per second

frame_rate.description: Frame rate as string to preserve precision (e.g., '23.976', '29.97', '30')

frame_rate_type.description: Whether the video uses constant (CFR) or variable (VFR) frame rate

hdr_format.description: HDR format if applicable, or 'sdr' for standard dynamic range

moov_atom_position.description: Position of moov atom in MP4 container

has_audio.description: Whether the video contains an audio track

audio_codec.description: Audio codec used (aac, aac_lc, he_aac, pcm, mp3, ac3, eac3, etc.)

audio_bitrate_kbps.description: Audio bitrate in kilobits per second

captions_url.description: URL to captions file (WebVTT, SRT, etc.)

transcript_url.description: URL to text transcript of the video content

audio_description_url.description: URL to audio description track for visually impaired users

min_duration_ms.description: Minimum display duration in milliseconds for initial persistence. Recommended when persistence is initial — without it, the duration is at the publisher's discretion. At serve time the publisher reads this from provenance since the brief is not available.

positions.description: Preferred disclosure positions in priority order. The first position a format supports should be used.

items.title: Disclosure Position

Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

verification.description: Third-party verification or detection results for this content. Multiple services may independently evaluate the same content. Provenance is a claim — verification results attached by the declaring party are supplementary. The enforcing party (e.g., seller/publisher) should run its own verification via get_creative_features or calibrate_content.

verified_by.description: Name of the verification service (e.g., 'DoubleVerify', 'Hive Moderation', 'Reality Defender')

confidence.description: Confidence score of the verification result (0.0 to 1.0)

details_url.description: URL to the full verification report

delivery_type.description: Discriminator indicating DAAST is delivered as inline XML content

duration_ms.description: Expected audio duration in milliseconds (if known)

items.title: DAAST Tracking Event
items.description: Standard DAAST tracking events for audio ad playback and interaction

tracking_events.description: Tracking events supported by this DAAST tag

companion_ads.description: Whether companion display ads are included

transcript_url.description: URL to text transcript of the audio content

provenance.title: Provenance
provenance.description: Provenance metadata for this asset, overrides manifest-level provenance

digital_source_type.title: Digital Source Type
digital_source_type.description: IPTC-aligned classification of AI involvement in producing this content

enumDescriptions.digital_capture: Captured by a digital device (camera, scanner, screen recording) with no AI involvement
enumDescriptions.digital_creation: Created by a human using digital tools (Photoshop, Illustrator, After Effects) without AI generation
enumDescriptions.trained_algorithmic_media: Generated entirely by a trained AI model (DALL-E, Midjourney, Stable Diffusion, Sora)
enumDescriptions.composite_with_trained_algorithmic_media: Human-created content combined with AI-generated elements (e.g., photo with AI background)
enumDescriptions.algorithmic_media: Produced by deterministic algorithms without machine learning (procedural generation, rule-based systems)
enumDescriptions.composite_capture: Multiple digital captures composited together without AI
enumDescriptions.composite_synthetic: Composite of multiple elements where at least one is AI-generated (e.g., stock photo composited with AI-generated background)
enumDescriptions.human_edits: Content augmented, corrected, or enhanced by humans using non-generative tools
enumDescriptions.data_driven_media: Assembled from structured data feeds (DCO templates, product catalogs, weather-triggered variants)

ai_tool.description: AI system used to generate or modify this content. Aligns with IPTC 2025.1 AI metadata fields and C2PA claim_generator.

name.description: Name of the AI tool or model (e.g., 'DALL-E 3', 'Stable Diffusion XL', 'Gemini')

version.description: Version identifier for the AI tool or model (e.g., '25.1', '0125', '2.1'). For generative models, use the model version rather than the API version.

provider.description: Organization that provides the AI tool (e.g., 'OpenAI', 'Stability AI', 'Google')

human_oversight.description: Level of human involvement in the AI-assisted creation process

enumDescriptions.none: Fully automated with no human involvement in generation
enumDescriptions.prompt_only: Human provided the prompt or instructions but did not review outputs
enumDescriptions.selected: Human selected from multiple AI-generated candidates
enumDescriptions.edited: Human edited or refined AI-generated output
enumDescriptions.directed: Human directed the creative process with AI as an assistive tool

declared_by.description: Party declaring this provenance. Identifies who attached the provenance claim, enabling receiving parties to assess trust.

agent_url.description: URL of the agent or service that declared this provenance

role.description: Role of the declaring party in the supply chain

enumDescriptions.creator: The party that created or generated the content
enumDescriptions.advertiser: The brand or advertiser that owns the content
enumDescriptions.agency: Agency acting on behalf of the advertiser
enumDescriptions.platform: Ad platform or publisher that processed the content
enumDescriptions.tool: Automated tool or service that attached provenance metadata

declared_at.description: When this provenance claim was made (ISO 8601). Distinct from created_time, which records when the content itself was produced. A provenance claim may be attached well after content creation, for example when retroactively declaring AI involvement for regulatory compliance.

created_time.description: When this content was created or generated (ISO 8601)

c2pa.description: C2PA Content Credentials reference. Links to the cryptographic provenance manifest for this content. Because file-level C2PA bindings break during ad-tech transcoding, this URL reference preserves the chain of provenance through the supply chain.

manifest_url.description: URL to the C2PA manifest store for this content

disclosure.description: Regulatory disclosure requirements for this content. Indicates whether AI disclosure is required and under which jurisdictions.

required.description: Whether AI disclosure is required for this content based on applicable regulations

region.description: Sub-national region code (e.g., 'CA' for California, 'BY' for Bavaria)

label_text.description: Required disclosure label text for this jurisdiction, in the local language

render_guidance.description: How the disclosure should be rendered for this jurisdiction. Expresses the declaring party's intent for persistence and position based on regulatory requirements. Publishers control actual rendering but governance agents can audit whether guidance was followed.

persistence.title: Disclosure Persistence
persistence.description: How long the disclosure must persist during content playback or display

enumDescriptions.continuous: Disclosure must remain visible or audible throughout the entire content display duration. For video and audio, this means the full playback duration. For static formats (display, DOOH), this means the full display slot. For DOOH specifically, 'content duration' means the ad's display slot within the rotation, not the screen's full rotation cycle.
enumDescriptions.initial: Disclosure must appear at the start of content for a minimum duration before it may be removed. Pair with min_duration_ms in render_guidance or creative brief to specify the required duration.
enumDescriptions.flexible: Disclosure presence is sufficient; placement timing and duration are at the publisher's discretion

min_duration_ms.description: Minimum display duration in milliseconds for initial persistence. Recommended when persistence is initial — without it, the duration is at the publisher's discretion. At serve time the publisher reads this from provenance since the brief is not available.

positions.description: Preferred disclosure positions in priority order. The first position a format supports should be used.

items.description: Where a required disclosure should appear within a creative. Used by creative briefs to specify disclosure placement and by formats to declare which positions they can render.

anyOf.title: Markdown Asset
anyOf.description: Markdown-formatted text content following CommonMark specification

content.description: Markdown content following CommonMark spec with optional GitHub Flavored Markdown extensions

markdown_flavor.title: Markdown Flavor
markdown_flavor.description: Markdown flavor used. CommonMark for strict compatibility, GFM for tables/task lists/strikethrough.

allow_raw_html.description: Whether raw HTML blocks are allowed in the markdown. False recommended for security.

anyOf.title: Brief Asset
anyOf.description: Campaign-level creative context as an asset. Carries the creative brief through the manifest so it travels with the creative through regeneration, resizing, and auditing.

allOf.title: Creative Brief
allOf.description: Campaign-level creative context for AI-powered creative generation. Provides the layer between brand identity (stable across campaigns) and individual creative execution (per-request). A brand has one identity (defined in brand.json) but different creative briefs for each campaign or flight.

name.description: Campaign or flight name for identification

objective.description: Campaign objective that guides creative tone and call-to-action strategy

tone.description: Desired tone for this campaign, modulating the brand's base tone (e.g., 'playful and festive', 'premium and aspirational')

audience.description: Target audience description for this campaign

territory.description: Creative territory or positioning the campaign should occupy

key_messages.description: Key messages to communicate in priority order

reference_assets.description: Visual and strategic reference materials such as mood boards, product shots, example creatives, and strategy documents

items.title: Reference Asset
items.description: A reference asset that provides creative context. Carries visual materials (mood boards, product shots, example creatives) with semantic roles that tell creative agents how to use them.

url.description: URL to the reference asset (image, video, or document)

role.description: How the creative agent should use this asset. style_reference: match the visual style; product_shot: include this product; mood_board: overall look and feel; example_creative: example of a similar execution; logo: logo to use; strategy_doc: strategy or planning document for context; storyboard: sequential visual direction for video or multi-scene creative

description.description: Human-readable description of the asset and how it should inform creative generation

compliance.description: Regulatory and legal compliance requirements for this campaign. Campaign-specific, regional, and product-based — distinct from brand-level disclaimers in brand.json.

required_disclosures.description: Disclosures that must appear in creatives for this campaign. Each disclosure specifies the text, where it should appear, and which jurisdictions require it.

text.description: The disclosure text that must appear in the creative

position.title: Disclosure Position
position.description: Where the disclosure should appear within the creative. prominent: clearly visible in the main creative area; footer: at the bottom of visual creatives; audio: spoken in audio/video creatives; subtitle: displayed in the subtitle or closed-caption track; overlay: superimposed on video content; end_card: displayed on video end card; pre_roll: spoken or displayed before main content; companion: in companion ad unit alongside primary creative

jurisdictions.description: Jurisdictions where this disclosure is required. ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes or ISO 3166-2 subdivision codes (e.g., 'US', 'GB', 'US-NJ', 'CA-QC'). If omitted, the disclosure applies to all jurisdictions in the campaign.

regulation.description: The regulation or legal authority requiring this disclosure (e.g., 'SEC Rule 156', 'FCA COBS 4.5', 'FDA 21 CFR 202')

min_duration_ms.description: Minimum display duration in milliseconds. For video/audio disclosures, how long the disclosure must be visible or audible. For static formats, how long the disclosure must remain on screen before any auto-advance.

language.description: Language of the disclosure text as a BCP 47 language tag (e.g., 'en', 'fr-CA', 'es'). When omitted, the disclosure is assumed to match the creative's language.

persistence.title: Disclosure Persistence
persistence.description: How long the disclosure must persist during content playback or display. When omitted, the creative agent should use the most restrictive persistence mode supported by the target format.

enumDescriptions.continuous: Disclosure must remain visible or audible throughout the entire content display duration. For video and audio, this means the full playback duration. For static formats (display, DOOH), this means the full display slot. For DOOH specifically, 'content duration' means the ad's display slot within the rotation, not the screen's full rotation cycle.
enumDescriptions.initial: Disclosure must appear at the start of content for a minimum duration before it may be removed. Pair with min_duration_ms in render_guidance or creative brief to specify the required duration.
enumDescriptions.flexible: Disclosure presence is sufficient; placement timing and duration are at the publisher's discretion

prohibited_claims.description: Claims that must not appear in creatives for this campaign. Creative agents should ensure generated content avoids these claims.

anyOf.title: Catalog Asset
anyOf.description: A typed data feed as a creative asset. Carries catalog context (products, stores, jobs, etc.) within the manifest's assets map.

allOf.title: Catalog
allOf.description: A typed data feed. Catalogs carry the items, locations, stock levels, or pricing that publishers use to render ads. They can be synced to a platform via sync_catalogs (managed lifecycle with approval), provided inline, or fetched from an external URL. The catalog type determines the item schema and can be structural (offering, product, inventory, store, promotion) or vertical-specific (hotel, flight, job, vehicle, real_estate, education, destination, app). Selectors (ids, tags, category, query) filter items regardless of sourcing method.

catalog_id.description: Buyer's identifier for this catalog. Required when syncing via sync_catalogs. When used in creatives, references a previously synced catalog on the account.

name.description: Human-readable name for this catalog (e.g., 'Summer Products 2025', 'Amsterdam Store Locations').

type.title: Catalog Type
type.description: Catalog type. Structural types: 'offering' (AdCP Offering objects), 'product' (ecommerce entries), 'inventory' (stock per location), 'store' (physical locations), 'promotion' (deals and pricing). Vertical types: 'hotel', 'flight', 'job', 'vehicle', 'real_estate', 'education', 'destination', 'app' — each with an industry-specific item schema.

url.description: URL to an external catalog feed. The platform fetches and resolves items from this URL. For offering-type catalogs, the feed contains an array of Offering objects. For other types, the feed format is determined by feed_format. When omitted with type 'product', the platform uses its synced copy of the brand's product catalog.

feed_format.title: Feed Format
feed_format.description: Format of the external feed at url. Required when url points to a non-AdCP feed (e.g., Google Merchant Center XML, Meta Product Catalog). Omit for offering-type catalogs where the feed is native AdCP JSON.

update_frequency.title: Update Frequency
update_frequency.description: How often the platform should re-fetch the feed from url. Only applicable when url is provided. Platforms may use this as a hint for polling schedules.

items.description: Inline catalog data. The item schema depends on the catalog type: Offering objects for 'offering', StoreItem for 'store', HotelItem for 'hotel', FlightItem for 'flight', JobItem for 'job', VehicleItem for 'vehicle', RealEstateItem for 'real_estate', EducationItem for 'education', DestinationItem for 'destination', AppItem for 'app', or freeform objects for 'product', 'inventory', and 'promotion'. Mutually exclusive with url — provide one or the other, not both. Implementations should validate items against the type-specific schema.

ids.description: Filter catalog to specific item IDs. For offering-type catalogs, these are offering_id values. For product-type catalogs, these are SKU identifiers.

gtins.description: Filter product-type catalogs by GTIN identifiers for cross-retailer catalog matching. Accepts standard GTIN formats (GTIN-8, UPC-A/GTIN-12, EAN-13/GTIN-13, GTIN-14). Only applicable when type is 'product'.

tags.description: Filter catalog to items with these tags. Tags are matched using OR logic — items matching any tag are included.

category.description: Filter catalog to items in this category (e.g., 'beverages/soft-drinks', 'chef-positions').

query.description: Natural language filter for catalog items (e.g., 'all pasta sauces under $5', 'amsterdam vacancies').

conversion_events.description: Event types that represent conversions for items in this catalog. Declares what events the platform should attribute to catalog items — e.g., a job catalog converts via submit_application, a product catalog via purchase. The event's content_ids field carries the item IDs that connect back to catalog items. Use content_id_type to declare what identifier type content_ids values represent.

items.title: Event Type
items.description: Standard marketing event types for event logging, aligned with IAB ECAPI

enumDescriptions.view_content: User viewed specific content (product, article, etc.)
enumDescriptions.select_content: User selected or clicked on content (article, video, etc.)
enumDescriptions.select_item: User selected a specific product or item from a list
enumDescriptions.share: User shared content via social or messaging
enumDescriptions.add_to_cart: User added an item to cart
enumDescriptions.remove_from_cart: User removed an item from cart
enumDescriptions.add_to_wishlist: User added an item to a wishlist
enumDescriptions.refund: A purchase was fully or partially refunded (adjusts ROAS)
enumDescriptions.lead: User expressed interest (form submission, signup, etc.)
enumDescriptions.qualify_lead: Lead qualified by sales or scoring criteria
enumDescriptions.close_convert_lead: Lead converted to a customer or closed deal
enumDescriptions.disqualify_lead: Lead disqualified or marked as not viable
enumDescriptions.subscribe: User subscribed to a service or newsletter
enumDescriptions.schedule: User scheduled an appointment or event
enumDescriptions.submit_application: User submitted an application (loan, job, etc.)

content_id_type.title: Content ID Type
content_id_type.description: Identifier type that the event's content_ids field should be matched against for items in this catalog. For example, 'gtin' means content_ids values are Global Trade Item Numbers, 'sku' means retailer SKUs. Omit when using a custom identifier scheme not listed in the enum.

feed_field_mappings.description: Declarative normalization rules for external feeds. Maps non-standard feed field names, date formats, price encodings, and image URLs to the AdCP catalog item schema. Applied during sync_catalogs ingestion. Supports field renames, named transforms (date, divide, boolean, split), static literal injection, and assignment of image URLs to typed asset pools.

items.title: Catalog Field Mapping
items.description: Declares how a field in an external feed maps to the AdCP catalog item schema. Used in sync_catalogs feed_field_mappings to normalize non-AdCP feeds (Google Merchant Center, LinkedIn Jobs XML, hotel XML, etc.) to the standard catalog item schema without requiring the buyer to preprocess every feed. Multiple mappings can assemble a nested object via dot notation (e.g., separate mappings for price.amount and price.currency).

feed_field.description: Field name in the external feed record. Omit when injecting a static literal value (use the value property instead).

catalog_field.description: Target field on the catalog item schema, using dot notation for nested fields (e.g., 'name', 'price.amount', 'location.city'). Mutually exclusive with asset_group_id.

asset_group_id.description: Places the feed field value (a URL) into a typed asset pool on the catalog item's assets array. The value is wrapped as an image or video asset in a group with this ID. Use standard group IDs: 'images_landscape', 'images_vertical', 'images_square', 'logo', 'video'. Mutually exclusive with catalog_field.

value.description: Static literal value to inject into catalog_field for every item, regardless of what the feed contains. Mutually exclusive with feed_field. Useful for fields the feed omits (e.g., currency when price is always USD, or a constant category value).

transform.description: Named transform to apply to the feed field value before writing to the catalog schema. See transform-specific parameters (format, timezone, by, separator).

format.description: For transform 'date': the input date format string (e.g., 'YYYYMMDD', 'MM/DD/YYYY', 'DD-MM-YYYY'). Output is always ISO 8601 (e.g., '2025-03-01'). Uses Unicode date pattern tokens.

timezone.description: For transform 'date': the timezone of the input value. IANA timezone identifier (e.g., 'UTC', 'America/New_York', 'Europe/Amsterdam'). Defaults to UTC when omitted.

by.description: For transform 'divide': the divisor to apply (e.g., 100 to convert integer cents to decimal dollars).

separator.description: For transform 'split': the separator character or string to split on. Defaults to ','.

default.description: Fallback value to use when feed_field is absent, null, or empty. Applied after any transform would have been applied. Allows optional feed fields to have a guaranteed baseline value.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

tags.description: User-defined tags for organization and searchability

concept_id.description: Creative concept this creative belongs to. Concepts group related creatives across sizes and formats.

variables.description: Dynamic content variables (DCO slots) for this creative. Included when include_variables=true.

items.title: Creative Variable
items.description: A dynamic content variable (DCO slot) on a creative. Variables represent content that can change at serve time — headlines, images, product data, etc.

variable_id.description: Variable identifier on the creative platform

variable_type.description: Data type of the variable. Each type represents a semantic content slot: text (headlines, body copy), image/video/audio (media URLs), url (clickthrough or tracking URLs), number (prices, counts), boolean (conditional flags like show_discount or is_raining), color (hex color values), date (ISO 8601 date-time for countdowns and offer expirations).

default_value.description: Default value used when no dynamic value is provided at serve time. All types are string-encoded: text/image/video/audio/url as literal strings, number as decimal (e.g., "42.99"), boolean as "true"/"false", color as "#RRGGBB", date as ISO 8601 (e.g., "2026-12-25T00:00:00Z").

required.description: Whether this variable must have a value for the creative to serve

assignment_count.description: Total number of active package assignments

assigned_packages.description: List of packages this creative is assigned to

snapshot.description: Lightweight delivery snapshot (included when include_snapshot=true). For detailed performance analytics, use get_creative_delivery.

as_of.description: When this snapshot was captured by the platform

staleness_seconds.description: Maximum age of this data in seconds. For example, 3600 means the data may be up to 1 hour old.

impressions.description: Lifetime impressions across all assignments. Not scoped to any date range.

last_served.description: Last time this creative served an impression. Absent when the creative has never served.

snapshot_unavailable_reason.description: Machine-readable reason the snapshot is omitted. Present only when include_snapshot was true and snapshot data is unavailable for this creative.

items.description: Items for multi-asset formats like carousels and native ads (included when include_items=true)

items.title: Creative Item
items.description: Item within a multi-asset creative format. Used for carousel products, native ad components, and other formats composed of multiple distinct elements.

asset_kind.description: Discriminator indicating this is a media asset with content_uri

asset_type.description: Type of asset. Common types: thumbnail_image, product_image, featured_image, logo

asset_id.description: Unique identifier for the asset within the creative

content_uri.description: URL for media assets (images, videos, etc.)

asset_kind.description: Discriminator indicating this is a text asset with content

asset_type.description: Type of asset. Common types: headline, body_text, cta_text, price_text, sponsor_name, author_name, click_url

asset_id.description: Unique identifier for the asset within the creative

oneOf.description: Multiple text content values (for A/B testing or variations)

content.description: Text content for text-based assets like headlines, body text, CTA text, etc.

pricing_options.description: Pricing options for using this creative (serving, delivery). Used by ad servers and library agents. Transformation agents expose format-level pricing on list_creative_formats instead. Present when include_pricing=true and account provided. The buyer passes the applied pricing_option_id in report_usage.

items.title: Vendor Pricing Option
items.description: A pricing option offered by a vendor agent (signals, creative, governance). Combines pricing_option_id with the pricing model fields. Pass pricing_option_id in report_usage for billing verification. All vendor discovery responses return pricing_options as an array — vendors may offer multiple options (volume tiers, context-specific rates, different models per product line).

pricing_option_id.description: Opaque identifier for this pricing option, unique within the vendor agent. Pass this in report_usage to identify which pricing option was applied.

allOf.title: Vendor Pricing
allOf.description: Pricing model for a vendor service. Discriminated by model: 'cpm' (fixed CPM), 'percent_of_media' (percentage of spend with optional CPM cap), 'flat_fee' (fixed charge per reporting period), or 'per_unit' (fixed price per unit of work).

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

oneOf.title: PercentOfMediaPricing
oneOf.description: Percentage of media spend charged for this signal. When max_cpm is set, the effective rate is capped at that CPM — useful for platforms like The Trade Desk that use percent-of-media pricing with a CPM ceiling.

max_cpm.description: Optional CPM cap. When set, the effective charge is min(percent × media_spend_per_mille, max_cpm).

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for the resulting charge

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

oneOf.title: FlatFeePricing
oneOf.description: Fixed charge per billing period, regardless of impressions or spend. Used for licensed data bundles and audience subscriptions.

amount.description: Fixed charge for the billing period

period.description: Billing period for the flat fee.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

oneOf.title: PerUnitPricing
oneOf.description: Fixed price per unit of work. Used for creative transformation (per format), AI generation (per image, per token), and rendering (per variant). The unit field describes what is counted; unit_price is the cost per one unit.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

format_summary.description: Breakdown of creatives by format. Keys are agent-defined format identifiers, optionally including dimensions (e.g., 'display_static_300x250', 'video_30s_vast'). Key construction is platform-specific — there is no required format.

^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$.description: Number of creatives with this format

errors.description: Task-specific errors (e.g., invalid filters, account not found)

items.title: Error
items.description: Standard error structure for task-specific errors and warnings

code.description: Error code for programmatic handling. Standard codes are defined in error-code.json and enable autonomous agent recovery. Sellers MAY use codes not in the standard vocabulary for platform-specific errors; agents MUST handle unknown codes gracefully by falling back to the recovery classification.

field.description: Field path associated with the error (e.g., 'packages[0].targeting')

retry_after.description: Seconds to wait before retrying the operation. Sellers MUST return values between 1 and 3600. Clients MUST clamp values outside this range.

recovery.description: Agent recovery classification. transient: retry after delay (rate limit, service unavailable, timeout). correctable: fix the request and resend (invalid field, budget too low, creative rejected). terminal: requires human action (account suspended, payment required, account not found).

sandbox.description: When true, this response contains simulated data from sandbox mode.

context.title: Context Object
context.description: Opaque correlation data that is echoed unchanged in responses. Used for internal tracking, UI session IDs, trace IDs, and other caller-specific identifiers that don't affect protocol behavior. Context data is never parsed by AdCP agents - it's simply preserved and returned.

ext.title: Extension Object
ext.description: Extension object for platform-specific, vendor-namespaced parameters. Extensions are always optional and must be namespaced under a vendor/platform key (e.g., ext.gam, ext.roku). Used for custom capabilities, partner-specific configuration, and features being proposed for standardization.

examples.description: Library query with creatives and variables

_bundled.note: This is a bundled schema with all $ref resolved inline. For the modular version with references, use the parent directory.
